description:
Establish the complete data persistence layer for the underwriting-service. This epic begins with a structural fix story that resolves a nested directory layout, inconsistent profile file extensions, and a non-standard package name — all of which must be resolved before data layer work can proceed. It then covers service profile configuration, Flyway-managed schema creation for underwriting_cases, underwriting_checks, underwriting_decisions, and manual_review_notes, JPA entity mapping, repository setup, and the test infrastructure needed to validate migration and entity alignment without requiring a running database.

The processed_events table is intentionally excluded from this epic — it supports Kafka idempotent consumption and belongs to the event-driven workflow epic.

Stories in this epic are sourced from:
- story-underwriting-scaffold-fix.json
- story-underwriting-application-properties.json
- stories-underwriting-schema.json (migrations, UnderwritingCase and Check entities, UnderwritingDecision and ManualReviewNote entities)

The Docker Compose MySQL infrastructure story is excluded — it is tracked under a separate infrastructure epic.
userType: backend developer owning the Underwriting and Decision Service
functionality: fix the service scaffold structure and configure the data layer including database connectivity, schema migrations, JPA entities for underwriting cases, checks, decisions, and manual review notes, and test infrastructure
benefit: so the underwriting service has a structurally sound, validated persistence foundation that the underwriting rules engine, Kafka consumers, and decision workflow features can build on without schema drift between environments

stories.summary: Configure application properties across dev and test profiles for underwriting-service
stories.description:
Following resolution of the scaffold structure fix story, this story adds the required Maven dependencies for persistence, migration, and validation, defines environment variables for the underwriting database, and populates application-dev.yaml and application-test.yaml so the service can start, connect to MySQL, run Flyway migrations, and run tests in isolation against H2 without requiring a running database instance.

Note: infra/mysql/init/01_init.sql must be updated to provision the loanflow_underwriting_db database and grant the application user access. This depends on the Docker Compose MySQL infrastructure story.

Note: processed_events table is excluded from this epic — it is part of the Kafka idempotency pattern introduced in the event-driven workflow epic.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Service starts and runs Flyway migrations on dev profile
acceptanceCriteria.given: the MySQL container is running and environment variables are set from .env
acceptanceCriteria.when: the underwriting-service starts with the dev profile
acceptanceCriteria.then: Flyway runs migration scripts from classpath:db/migration against loanflow_underwriting_db and JPA validates the schema without attempting to auto-create tables

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Tests run Flyway migrations against H2 and validate entity mappings
acceptanceCriteria.given: the test profile is active and no MySQL instance is running
acceptanceCriteria.then: Flyway runs all migration scripts against H2 in-memory, Hibernate validates entity mappings against the migrated schema, and the context loads without error

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: No credentials are hardcoded in source
acceptanceCriteria.then: all connection values are resolved from environment variables with inline defaults and no plaintext credentials appear in committed files

tasks.summary: Add JPA, MySQL, Flyway, Validation, and H2 dependencies to pom.xml
tasks.description:
Add the following dependencies to the underwriting-service pom.xml:

- spring-boot-starter-data-jpa
- spring-boot-starter-validation
- mysql-connector-j (runtime scope)
- flyway-core
- com.h2database:h2 (test scope only)

Ensure versions are compatible with Spring Boot 3.5.13.

tasks.summary: Add underwriting database environment variables to infra/.env.example
Configure datasource, JPA, and Flyway in application-dev.yaml
tasks.description:
Populate application-dev.yaml with server port, datasource, JPA, and Flyway settings. Each connection part must use environment variable substitution with inline defaults:

server:
  port: 8084

spring:
  datasource:
    url: jdbc:mysql://${DATABASE_HOST:localhost}:${DATABASE_PORT:3306}/${UNDERWRITING_DB_NAME:loanflow_underwriting_db}
    username: ${DATABASE_USER:loanflow}
    password: ${DATABASE_PASSWORD:password}
  jpa:
    hibernate:
      ddl-auto: validate
    show-sql: true
    properties:
      hibernate:
        dialect: org.hibernate.dialect.MySQLDialect
        format_sql: true
  flyway:
    enabled: true
    locations: classpath:db/migration

tasks.summary: Configure application-test.yaml to run Flyway against H2 with schema validation
tasks.description:
Populate application-test.yaml so tests run Flyway migrations against H2 in-memory and validate entity mappings against the migrated schema without requiring MySQL or Docker:

spring:
  datasource:
    url: jdbc:h2:mem:loanflow_underwriting_test;DB_CLOSE_DELAY=-1;MODE=MySQL;NON_KEYWORDS=VALUE
    driver-class-name: org.h2.Driver
    username: sa
    password:
  jpa:
    hibernate:
      ddl-auto: validate
    show-sql: true
  flyway:
    enabled: true
    locations: classpath:db/migration

Note: migration SQL must be written to the H2-compatible subset. Avoid ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP and ENGINE=InnoDB.

stories.summary: Create Flyway migration scripts for underwriting service schema
stories.description: Write the SQL migration scripts that define the underwriting service database schema across four tables: underwriting_cases, underwriting_checks, underwriting_decisions, and manual_review_notes. The processed_events table is excluded — it belongs to the Kafka idempotency pattern introduced in the event-driven workflow epic. Migrations run in order via Flyway against MySQL 8 in dev and against H2 in MySQL mode in the test profile. All SQL must be written to the common subset supported by both targets: avoid ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, ENGINE=InnoDB, and MySQL-specific index syntax not supported by H2.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Flyway runs all migrations cleanly on dev startup
acceptanceCriteria.given: the MySQL container is running and the underwriting-service starts with the dev profile
acceptanceCriteria.then: all four migration scripts apply in order without errors and the flyway_schema_history table records each as success

acceptanceCriteria.when: the underwriting-service starts with ddl-auto set to validate
acceptanceCriteria.then: Hibernate validates all entity mappings against the schema without throwing a validation exception

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Migrations execute against H2 in test profile and entities validate
acceptanceCriteria.given: the test profile is active with Flyway enabled against H2 and ddl-auto set to validate
acceptanceCriteria.then: Flyway runs all migration scripts against H2 without error and Hibernate validates all entity mappings against the migrated schema

tasks.description:
Define the underwriting_cases table:

- underwriting_case_id BIGINT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY
- application_id BIGINT NOT NULL
- case_status VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL
- decision_status VARCHAR(50) NULL
- decision_reason VARCHAR(500) NULL
- manual_review_required BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE
- created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
- completed_at TIMESTAMP NULL

Constraints:
- UNIQUE on application_id (one active case per application)

Indexes:
- INDEX on case_status
- INDEX on decision_status

H2 compatibility: omit ENGINE=InnoDB and DEFAULT CHARSET clauses. Do not use ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP.

Define the underwriting_checks table:

- check_id BIGINT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY
- underwriting_case_id BIGINT NOT NULL
- check_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL
- check_status VARCHAR(30) NOT NULL
- result_code VARCHAR(50) NULL
- result_summary VARCHAR(255) NULL
- executed_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP

Constraints:
- FOREIGN KEY (underwriting_case_id) REFERENCES underwriting_cases(underwriting_case_id)

Indexes:
- INDEX on underwriting_case_id
- INDEX on check_type

H2 compatibility: omit ENGINE=InnoDB and DEFAULT CHARSET clauses.

Define the underwriting_decisions table. Keep as one-to-many to preserve re-evaluation and manual override history:

- decision_id BIGINT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY
- underwriting_case_id BIGINT NOT NULL
- decision_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL
- recommended_amount DECIMAL(15,2) NULL
- recommended_term_months INT NULL
- decision_source VARCHAR(30) NOT NULL
- decided_by_user_id BIGINT NULL
- decision_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP

Constraints:
- FOREIGN KEY (underwriting_case_id) REFERENCES underwriting_cases(underwriting_case_id)

Indexes:
- INDEX on underwriting_case_id
- INDEX on decision_at

H2 compatibility: omit ENGINE=InnoDB and DEFAULT CHARSET clauses.

Define the manual_review_notes table:

- review_note_id BIGINT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY
- underwriting_case_id BIGINT NOT NULL
- reviewer_user_id BIGINT NOT NULL
- note_text VARCHAR(1000) NOT NULL
- created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP

Constraints:
- FOREIGN KEY (underwriting_case_id) REFERENCES underwriting_cases(underwriting_case_id)

Indexes:
- INDEX on underwriting_case_id
- INDEX on created_at

H2 compatibility: omit ENGINE=InnoDB and DEFAULT CHARSET clauses.

stories.summary: Create UnderwritingCase and UnderwritingCheck JPA entities and repositories
stories.description: Implement the UnderwritingCase and UnderwritingCheck JPA entities mapped to their respective tables, along with supporting enums and Spring Data repositories. UnderwritingCase is the aggregate root for the underwriting service — all checks, decisions, and review notes belong to a case. All field names, column mappings, and constraints must align with the V1 and V2 Flyway migrations.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: UnderwritingCase and UnderwritingCheck validate against migrated schema
acceptanceCriteria.given: Flyway migrations have run and ddl-auto is set to validate
acceptanceCriteria.then: Hibernate validates both entities against their tables without error

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: A case can be persisted and its checks retrieved
acceptanceCriteria.when: UnderwritingCheck records are saved referencing the case and the case is reloaded
acceptanceCriteria.then: all associated checks are retrievable by underwriting_case_id

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Unique constraint prevents duplicate cases for the same application
acceptanceCriteria.given: an UnderwritingCase already exists for a given application_id
acceptanceCriteria.when: a second UnderwritingCase is saved with the same application_id

tasks.summary: Create CaseStatus, DecisionStatus, CheckType, and CheckStatus enums
tasks.description:
Create enums under com.loanflow.underwritingservice.entity.enums:

CaseStatus: OPEN, IN_PROGRESS, COMPLETED, FAILED

DecisionStatus: APPROVED, REJECTED, MANUAL_REVIEW

CheckType: DEBT_TO_INCOME, INCOME_SUFFICIENCY, FRAUD_FLAG, CREDIT_SCORE

CheckStatus: PENDING, PASSED, FAILED

Annotate with @Enumerated(EnumType.STRING) where used in entities.

Create com.loanflow.underwritingservice.entity.UnderwritingCase mapped to the underwriting_cases table:

- @Id @GeneratedValue underwritingCaseId
- applicationId (BIGINT, unique)
- caseStatus (@Enumerated STRING)
- decisionStatus (@Enumerated STRING, nullable)
- decisionReason (VARCHAR 500, nullable)
- manualReviewRequired (BOOLEAN, default false)
- createdAt (@CreationTimestamp, updatable = false)
- completedAt (nullable)
- checks: @OneToMany(mappedBy = "underwritingCase", fetch = LAZY, cascade = ALL)
- decisions: @OneToMany(mappedBy = "underwritingCase", fetch = LAZY, cascade = ALL)
- reviewNotes: @OneToMany(mappedBy = "underwritingCase", fetch = LAZY, cascade = ALL)

Annotate with @Entity, @Table(name = "underwriting_cases"), @Getter, @Setter, @NoArgsConstructor, @Builder.

Create com.loanflow.underwritingservice.entity.UnderwritingCheck mapped to underwriting_checks:

- @Id @GeneratedValue checkId
- @ManyToOne @JoinColumn(name = "underwriting_case_id") underwritingCase
- checkType (@Enumerated STRING)
- checkStatus (@Enumerated STRING)
- resultCode (VARCHAR 50, nullable)
- resultSummary (VARCHAR 255, nullable)
- executedAt (@CreationTimestamp, updatable = false)

Annotate with @Entity, @Table(name = "underwriting_checks"), @Getter, @Setter, @NoArgsConstructor, @Builder.

Create UnderwritingCaseRepository extending JpaRepository<UnderwritingCase, Long>:
- findByApplicationId(Long applicationId) returning Optional<UnderwritingCase>
- existsByApplicationId(Long applicationId) returning boolean
- findByCaseStatus(CaseStatus status) returning List<UnderwritingCase>

Create UnderwritingCheckRepository extending JpaRepository<UnderwritingCheck, Long>:
- findByUnderwritingCase_UnderwritingCaseId(Long caseId) returning List<UnderwritingCheck>
- findByUnderwritingCase_UnderwritingCaseIdAndCheckType(Long caseId, CheckType type) returning Optional<UnderwritingCheck>

stories.summary: Create UnderwritingDecision and ManualReviewNote JPA entities and repositories
stories.description: Implement the UnderwritingDecision and ManualReviewNote JPA entities mapped to their respective tables. UnderwritingDecision is modelled as one-to-many to preserve the full history of re-evaluations and manual overrides. ManualReviewNote is append-only. All mappings must align with the V3 and V4 Flyway migrations.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: UnderwritingDecision and ManualReviewNote validate against migrated schema
acceptanceCriteria.given: all four Flyway migrations have run and ddl-auto is validate
acceptanceCriteria.then: Hibernate validates both entities without error

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Multiple decisions are preserved for the same case
acceptanceCriteria.given: an UnderwritingCase has been through automated evaluation and a manual override
acceptanceCriteria.when: decisions are queried for the case
acceptanceCriteria.then: both decision records are returned ordered by decision_at, preserving full history

acceptanceCriteria.when: the repository is used to add a second note for the same case
acceptanceCriteria.then: both notes exist and neither is modified in place

tasks.description:
Create enums under com.loanflow.underwritingservice.entity.enums:

DecisionType: APPROVED, REJECTED, MANUAL_APPROVAL

DecisionSource: AUTOMATED, MANUAL_REVIEW

Annotate with @Enumerated(EnumType.STRING) where used in entities.

Create com.loanflow.underwritingservice.entity.UnderwritingDecision mapped to underwriting_decisions:

- @Id @GeneratedValue decisionId
- @ManyToOne @JoinColumn(name = "underwriting_case_id") underwritingCase
- decisionType (@Enumerated STRING)
- recommendedAmount (DECIMAL 15,2, nullable)
- recommendedTermMonths (INT, nullable)
- decisionSource (@Enumerated STRING)
- decidedByUserId (BIGINT, nullable — null for automated decisions)
- decisionAt (@CreationTimestamp, updatable = false)

Annotate with @Entity, @Table(name = "underwriting_decisions"), @Getter, @Setter, @NoArgsConstructor, @Builder.

Create UnderwritingDecisionRepository extending JpaRepository<UnderwritingDecision, Long>:
- findByUnderwritingCase_UnderwritingCaseIdOrderByDecisionAtAsc(Long caseId) returning List<UnderwritingDecision>
- findTopByUnderwritingCase_UnderwritingCaseIdOrderByDecisionAtDesc(Long caseId) returning Optional<UnderwritingDecision>

Create com.loanflow.underwritingservice.entity.ManualReviewNote mapped to manual_review_notes. This entity is append-only — no update operations should be performed:

- @Id @GeneratedValue reviewNoteId
- @ManyToOne @JoinColumn(name = "underwriting_case_id") underwritingCase
- reviewerUserId (BIGINT)
- noteText (VARCHAR 1000)
- createdAt (@CreationTimestamp, updatable = false)

Annotate with @Entity, @Table(name = "manual_review_notes"), @Getter, @NoArgsConstructor, @Builder.

Create ManualReviewNoteRepository extending JpaRepository<ManualReviewNote, Long>:
- findByUnderwritingCase_UnderwritingCaseIdOrderByCreatedAtAsc(Long caseId) returning List<ManualReviewNote>
